episodes.title: Bill Gates' Epstein testimony released; Gates claims Epstein made threats to expose affairs
episodes.description:
A transcript of the testimony that Bill Gates gave to a House committee about his relationship with notorious sex offender Jeffrey Epstein was released by the panel on Tuesday.

Gates, a co-founder of Microsoft, in his June 10 testimony, expressed regret about his contacts with Epstein, which he said were designed to win financial support for the Gates Foundation, his philanthropic venture.

“I should never have met with Epstein in the first place,” Gates told members of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee, according to the transcript.

Gates said he never saw Epstein participating in criminal conduct. Prosecutors have said the late money manager sexually abused scores of underage girls and young women.

The House panel also on Tuesday released a transcript of the June 9 testimony by Epstein’s former executive assistant, Lesley Groff.

Groff testified that she never saw any illegal conduct by Epstein, for whom she regularly scheduled what she said she believed were legitimate massages.

“Mr. Epstein was, in hindsight, a master manipulator and deceiver who separated his legitimate life from his secret life as an abuser,” Groff said.

“For 18 years, I worked for Dr. Jekyll, but was never permitted to see the true Mr. Hyde,” she said.

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world

episodes.title: Epstein files controversy shadows Bondi’s firing
episodes.description:
Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said that President Donald Trump’s decision to replace Pam Bondi didn’t have to do with how she handled the files related to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.

“I have never heard President Trump say anything that the attorney general was, that anything that happened to her had anything to do with the Epstein files,” Blanche said, according to The Hill newspaper.

Trump announced Thursday that he was replacing Bondi as attorney general and she would be transitioning to a "much-needed and important new job" in the private sector.

Bondi has gotten backlash from both Democrats and Republicans over how she handled the files related to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.

During a House Judiciary Committee hearing, California Democratic Rep. Ted Lieu accused Bondi of lying under oath about Trump’s relationship with Epstein.

“There are over a thousand sex trafficking victims and you have not held a single man accountable,” he said to Bondi during the hearing. “Shame on you. If you had any decency, you would resign right after this hearing.”

During a Fox News interview, Blanche defended how the administration handled the Epstein files.

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world/todd-blanche-says-trump-ousting-bondi-as-ag-had-nothing-to-do-with-epstein-files-democrats-republicans-justice-department

episodes.title: RAW: Comer gives update on Epstein probe, says no payments made to or from Trump
episodes.description:
Jeffrey Epstein’s former accountant Richard Kahn is testifying in a closed deposition before the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday.

Kahn managed the finances of Epstein for more than a decade and was the co-executor of his will. Epstein’s other co-executor, Darren Indyke, will be testifying before the committee on March 19.

Oversight Committee Chair James Comer (R-KY) told reporters as he walked in for the hearing Wednesday morning that questions would be centered around “how Epstein was able to accumulate so much wealth."

The late financier began his career as a math teacher at the Dalton School. He then moved on to a career in investment banking, and eventually began managing the portfolios for billionaires including Les Wexner, founder and CEO of L Brands; and Leon Black, Chairman of Apollo Global Management.

"It appears with the records we've obtained that there were somewhere around at least 64 entities affiliated with Epstein that we've discovered," said the Kentucky Republican.

Kahn has not been charged with any crimes.

"No one's accusing anyone of wrongdoing,” Comer said of Kahn. 

“I always have to say that because the lawyers watch all my interviews and any misstatement they would try to weasel out of answering questions.”

Kahn and Indyke were subpoenaed following the Justice Department releasing millions of documents.

Daniel H. Weiner, a lawyer for the men, told CBS News that Kahn and Indyke did not socialize with Epstein and had no knowledge of his illicit activities.

"It is worth emphasizing that not a single woman has ever accused either Mr. Indyke or Mr. Kahn of committing sexual abuse or witnessing sexual abuse, nor claimed at any time that she reported to them any allegation of Mr. Epstein's abuse," Weiner said.

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world/no-ones-accused-comer-says-as-epstein-accountant-faces-secret-house-deposition-epstein-files

episodes.title: Justice Department releases more Epstein files, including uncorroborated allegations against Trump
episodes.description:
The Justice Department on Thursday released additional Jeffrey Epstein files involving uncorroborated accusations made by a woman against President Donald Trump that the department said had been mistakenly withheld during an earlier review.

The department said last week that it was working to determine if any records were improperly withheld after several news organizations reported that the massive tranche of records that had been made public didn’t include some files documenting a series of interviews conducted in 2019 with a woman who made an allegation against Trump.

The accuser was interviewed by the FBI four times as it sought to assess her account but a summary of only one of those interviews had been included in the publicly released files.

On Thursday, the department said those files had been “incorrectly coded as duplicative,” and therefore were inadvertently not published along with other investigative documents related to the disgraced financier, who killed himself while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges in 2019.

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world/doj-publishes-missing-epstein-files-involving-uncorroborated-claim-about-trump

episodes.title: FULL: Hillary Clinton's deposition on Epstein ties
episodes.description:
The House Oversight Committee released the full depositions of former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton and former President Bill Clinton centered on the committee’s investigation into the Epstein files on Monday.

The release of their testimonies come after the pair separately testified before the committee in closed-door meetings last week. The footage shared showed that the couple faced more than four hours of questioning each.

“The American people have a lot of questions. To my knowledge, the Clintons haven't answered very many, if any, questions about their knowledge or involvement with Epstein and Maxwell,” Oversight Committee Chairman James Comer said Thursday before the deposition started.

“No one’s accusing, at this moment, the Clintons of any wrongdoing. They’re going to have due process."

Jeffrey Epstein was found guilty of sex trafficking minors in 2019 and died in prison that same year.

Ghislaine Maxwell, Epstein's associate, is currently serving time in prison. She was moved to a prison in Texas from Florida. Maxwell is currently serving a 20-year prison sentence after being convicted on child sex trafficking charges.

In her opening statement, Hillary accused the committee of using her and her husband as a distraction from President Donald Trump, who is heavily mentioned in the government documents.

"You have compelled me to testify, fully aware that I have no knowledge that would assist your investigation, in order to distract attention from President Trump's actions and cover them up despite legitimate calls for answers," she wrote in the statement.

"If this committee is serious about learning the truth about Epstein's trafficking crimes, it would not rely on press gaggles to get answers from our current president on his involvement; it would ask him directly under oath about the tens of thousands of times he shows up in the Epstein files," she continued.

WATCH BILL'S FULL TESTIMONY: https://youtu.be/Kud-yxVO3xM

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world/oversight-committee-posts-full-clinton-depositions-on-epstein-files-after-talks

episodes.title: FULL: Comer speaks after Bill Clinton's deposition in Epstein investigation
episodes.description:
Rep. James Comer held a press briefing Friday after former President Bill Clinton's closed-door testimony in Congress' investigation into late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

Clinton said he did nothing wrong and did not see any signs of Epstein's abuses in his opening statement to lawmakers.

Clinton’s deposition is the first time a former president has been compelled to testify before Congress. Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton sat with lawmakers for hours on Thursday.

“I had no idea of the crimes Epstein was committing. No matter how many photos you show me, I have two things at the end of the day matter more than your interpretation of those 20-year old photos,” he said in his opening statement. “I saw nothing, and I did nothing wrong.”

The former president has not been accused of wrongdoing but was pictured in several photos featured in the initial batch of Epstein files released by the Justice Department. Bill Clinton has also said he flew on Epstein’s plane while traveling abroad for the Clinton Foundation, which Republican lawmakers said would be a centerpiece of their long list of questions for him.

There were numerous photos of the former president with Epstein and co-conspirator Ghislaine Maxwell included in the DOJ’s files. He was photographed in a hot tub, in a swimming pool with Maxwell and sitting at a table with a woman whose identity was redacted. All of the photos are undated, and it is unclear where they were taken.

The former president has also said he stopped speaking with Epstein more than a decade before his 2019 arrest on sex trafficking charge. Epstein pleaded guilty to state charges of soliciting a minor in Florida in 2008 and died in jail awaiting trial on federal charges.

Bill Clinton said in his statement that he would tell the lawmakers he did not recall the specifics of events from more than two decades ago, but was confident he had not witnessed signs of Epstein's abuse.

READ MORE: https://13wham.com/news/nation-world/bill-clinton-to-testify-to-house-committee-in-jeffrey-epstein-investigation-ghislaine-maxwell-epstein-files-clinton-foundation-flights

episodes.title: DOJ withheld documents from Epstein files that included Trump accusations, House Democrat says
episodes.description:
A top Democrat on the House Oversight Committee accused the Department of Justice of withholding dozens of pages of information from the Jeffrey Epstein files, which he said include accusations against President Donald Trump.

California Rep. Robert Garcia pointed to what he said were dozens of pages of notes about a woman he said accused Trump of assaulting her when she was a minor in the 1980s.

episodes.title: Bill Gates cancels keynote address amid scrutiny over Epstein ties
episodes.description:
Bill Gates has pulled out of delivering his keynote address at an AI summit in India Thursday, his foundation said, as the billionaire faces scrutiny over his ties to late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

“After careful consideration, and to ensure the focus remains on the AI Summit’s key priorities, Mr. Gates will not be delivering his keynote address,” the Gates Foundation said in a statement just hours before he was due to speak.

No reason was provided for the abrupt withdrawal.

CNN has reached out to the Gates Foundation, the Gates Foundation India and the summit organizers for comment.

The Microsoft founder has come under fire in recent weeks over two recently released draft emails Epstein appears to have written to himself, in which he claims he facilitated sexual encounters for Gates and helped him obtain medication to hide a sexually transmitted infection from his wife.

It’s not clear who wrote the draft messages from 2013 saved in Epstein’s email account, but they are addressed from Epstein to himself.

Gates has strongly denied these claims as “false.”

The tech billionaire was due to speak at the AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, billed as one of the biggest AI conferences in the Global South. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the summit Thursday morning.

episodes.title: DOJ lists 300+ famous names in Epstein files, says no more files will be released
episodes.description:
The Department of Justice on Saturday sent Congress a list of “politically exposed persons” in the millions of files released related to its probes into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

The six-page letter, a copy of which was obtained by CNN, includes the names of many prominent figures who appear in the files, regardless of the context, and does not specify the degree to which the people were linked to or in contact with Epstein. The letter is signed by Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche and addressed to top members on the House and Senate Judiciary Committees.

The DOJ was compelled to produce the list by the Epstein Files Transparency Act passed by Congress. CNN has reached out to the DOJ for more details about the list it sent to lawmakers.

While some of the people on the list have well-documented ties to the convicted sex offender, others are not known to have ever personally interacted with Epstein. The list includes presidents, business leaders and government officials, in addition to cultural icons who have died, such as Princess Diana, Elvis Presley and Michael Jackson.

It also mentioned prominent individuals who have previously been linked to Epstein, including President Donald Trump, former President Bill Clinton, former Trump adviser Steve Bannon, former White House Counsel Kathy Ruemmler and billionaire business magnate Les Wexner.

No one on the list, other than Epstein and his accomplice Ghislane Maxwell, have ever been charged in connection with Epstein’s crimes.
